changed_functions.name: DampIoDispatch
key_changes: IOCTL 0x22a01c handler: Replaced ZwOpenProcess(PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS)+ObReferenceObjectByHandleWithTag with PsLookupProcessByProcessId — eliminates handle management and uses direct PID lookup

IOCTL 0x22a01c handler: ADDED ObfDereferenceObject(Process) in unified cleanup path — unpatched version NEVER dereferences the EPROCESS object obtained via ObReferenceObjectByHandleWithTag, leaking a reference on every IOCTL call

IOCTL 0x22a01c handler: ADDED NULL check on ExAllocatePoolWithTag return value — unpatched version uses the returned pointer directly in PsQueryProcessCommandLine without checking for NULL, causing a kernel NULL pointer dereference (BSOD) if allocation fails
IOCTL 0x22a01c handler: ADDED STATUS_NO_MEMORY (0xC0000017) error path when allocation fails — unpatched version falls through to PsQueryProcessCommandLine with NULL buffer

IOCTL 0x22a01c handler: MOVED ExFreePoolWithTag(P) and RtlFreeUnicodeString into unified cleanup section with NULL guards — unpatched version calls ExFreePoolWithTag unconditionally even when allocation returned NULL, and RtlFreeUnicodeString only in certain paths

Callee-saved register r14 replaced by r12 and the IRP pointer held in r15 instead of r14; stack frame shrinks 0xc0->0x70 because the OBJECT_ATTRIBUTES and CLIENT_ID locals are removed (PID passed directly to PsLookupProcessByProcessId) - cosmetic side effect of the restructured cleanup
changed_functions.security_notes:
Two security bugs in the IOCTL 0x22a01c handler (add process to DAM job object):

1. EPROCESS REFERENCE LEAK (CWE-404/911): After ObReferenceObjectByHandleWithTag succeeds, the obtained PEPROCESS (v24) is used throughout the handler but is NEVER dereferenced via ObfDereferenceObject. Only the handle is closed with ZwClose. Each successful IOCTL 0x22a01c call increments the EPROCESS reference count without decrementing it. Repeated calls prevent the process object from ever being freed, even after the process terminates. This is a resource exhaustion / object lifetime bug. The leaked reference also means the EPROCESS structure remains allocated indefinitely, which could be abused in combination with other vulnerabilities to maintain stale references to freed or reused kernel pool.

2. MISSING NULL CHECK ON POOL ALLOCATION (CWE-690/476): ExAllocatePoolWithTag(PagedPool, arg_10, 0x4b6d6144) is called to allocate a buffer for the process command line, but the return value (P) is immediately passed to PsQueryProcessCommandLine without checking for NULL. If the allocation fails (under memory pressure), this dereferences NULL in kernel mode, causing a bug check (BSOD). Additionally, ExFreePoolWithTag(P, ...) is called unconditionally afterward, which is also undefined behavior with a NULL pointer.

The patch fixes both by: (a) switching to PsLookupProcessByProcessId which manages its own reference counting semantics, (b) adding ObfDereferenceObject(Process) at cleanup, (c) adding NULL check on allocation with STATUS_NO_MEMORY return, (d) restructuring cleanup to a unified error-safe path with NULL guards.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
=== UNPATCHED vulnerable IOCTL 0x22a01c path (key instructions) ===

; ZwOpenProcess with 0x1FFFFF (PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS)
0x1c000a5c9: mov     esi, 1FFFFFh                 ; PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS
0x1c000a5ce: mov     eax, [rdi]                   ; PID (DWORD from SystemBuffer)
0x1c000a5d6: call    cs:__imp_ZwOpenProcess

; ObReferenceObjectByHandleWithTag - obtains the EPROCESS reference that is NEVER released
0x1c000a5fb: mov     edi, 4B6D6144h               ; tag 'DamK'
0x1c000a616: call    cs:__imp_ObReferenceObjectByHandleWithTag  ; PEPROCESS -> [rbp+57h+arg_10]

; ExAllocatePoolWithTag - NO NULL CHECK on the return
0x1c000a6b2: call    cs:__imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag            ; -> rax
0x1c000a6c9: mov     rsi, rax                     ; P (may be NULL)

; PsQueryProcessCommandLine uses P without a null check (NULL deref if alloc failed)
0x1c000a6d0: mov     rdx, rsi                     ; P
0x1c000a6d8: call    cs:__imp_PsQueryProcessCommandLine

; ExFreePoolWithTag called UNCONDITIONALLY (even if P == NULL)
0x1c000a6ef: call    cs:__imp_ExFreePoolWithTag

; ZwClose(Handle) - closes the handle but does NOT release the EPROCESS reference
0x1c000a628: mov     rcx, [rbp+57h+ProcessHandle] ; Handle
0x1c000a62c: call    cs:__imp_ZwClose
; *** MISSING: ObfDereferenceObject on the PEPROCESS - reference LEAKED ***

=== PATCHED fix (key instructions) ===

; PsLookupProcessByProcessId replaces the ZwOpenProcess + ObReference chain
0x1c000a5b5: mov     ecx, [rdi]                   ; PID from input buffer
0x1c000a5b7: lea     rdx, [rbp+Process]           ; &Process output
0x1c000a5bb: call    cs:__imp_PsLookupProcessByProcessId

; ExAllocatePoolWithTag WITH NULL CHECK
0x1c000a651: call    cs:__imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag
0x1c000a65d: mov     rsi, rax
0x1c000a660: test    rax, rax                     ; ADDED
0x1c000a663: jnz     short 0x1c000a66c            ; proceed only if non-NULL
0x1c000a665: mov     ebx, 0C0000017h              ; STATUS_NO_MEMORY

; ObfDereferenceObject(Process) - ADDED, guarded
0x1c000a6e1: mov     rcx, [rbp+Process]           ; Process
0x1c000a6e5: test    rcx, rcx                     ; NULL guard
0x1c000a6ea: call    cs:__imp_ObfDereferenceObject

; Conditional ExFreePoolWithTag (only if P != NULL) - GUARDED
0x1c000a6f6: test    rsi, rsi                     ; P != NULL ?
0x1c000a707: call    cs:__imp_ExFreePoolWithTag

security_findings.entry_point: DeviceIoControl to \\??\DamCtrl with IOCTL 0x22a01c (METHOD_BUFFERED, FILE_WRITE_ACCESS, Function 0x807)
call_chain: IOCTL 0x22a01c handler — ZwOpenProcess → ObReferenceObjectByHandleWithTag → ExAllocatePoolWithTag (no NULL check) → PsQueryProcessCommandLine → DampAddProcessToJobObject → cleanup (missing ObDereferenceObject)
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Kernel object reference leak (CWE-404) and NULL pointer dereference (CWE-690/476)
security_findings.description:
The IOCTL 0x22a01c handler in DampIoDispatch (the IRP_MJ_DEVICE_CONTROL dispatcher for the Desktop Activity Moderator driver dam.sys) has two security-relevant bugs:

1. EPROCESS REFERENCE LEAK: After successfully calling ObReferenceObjectByHandleWithTag to obtain a PEPROCESS object reference (v24), the handler uses the object throughout (PsGetProcessImageFileName, PsQueryProcessCommandLine, DampAddProcessToJobObject) but NEVER calls ObfDereferenceObject. Only the ZwOpenProcess handle is closed via ZwClose. The EPROCESS reference count is incremented but never decremented. Each IOCTL 0x22a01c call leaks one reference. This permanently prevents the process EPROCESS structure from being freed, even after the process exits.

2. NULL POINTER DEREFERENCE: The return value of ExAllocatePoolWithTag(PagedPool, arg_10, 'DamK') is stored in P and immediately passed to PsQueryProcessCommandLine(v24, P, arg_10, 0, &len) without any NULL check. If paged pool is exhausted and the allocation returns NULL, PsQueryProcessCommandLine will write to address 0x0 in kernel mode, causing a bug check (BSOD). ExFreePoolWithTag(P, 'DamK') is also called unconditionally afterward, which is additional undefined behavior with NULL.

The patch replaces the entire ZwOpenProcess + ObReferenceObjectByHandleWithTag sequence with PsLookupProcessByProcessId (simpler, self-managing reference), adds ObfDereferenceObject(Process) in a unified cleanup path, adds a NULL check on ExAllocatePoolWithTag returning STATUS_NO_MEMORY on failure, and restructures all cleanup to a single error-safe exit with NULL guards.
security_findings.trigger_conditions:
1. NULL POINTER DEREFERENCE (DoS): The attacker must open the \\??\DamCtrl device with FILE_WRITE_DATA access and send IOCTL 0x22a01c with an 8-byte input buffer where the first 4 bytes contain a valid process ID (HANDLE cast). The system must be under sufficient memory pressure that ExAllocatePoolWithTag(PagedPool, <cmdline_len>, 'DamK') returns NULL. This is most reliably triggered by first exhausting paged pool (e.g., via large allocations or memory pressure from many processes) then sending the IOCTL. The input buffer layout: bytes[0..3] = process ID (DWORD), bytes[4..7] = unused.

2. EPROCESS REFERENCE LEAK: Send IOCTL 0x22a01c repeatedly with a valid 8-byte input buffer (first DWORD = target PID). Each successful call leaks one EPROCESS reference. No special conditions needed beyond the IOCTL succeeding. Repeated calls against the same or different PIDs accumulate leaked references indefinitely.

NOTE: IOCTL 0x22a01c is NOT gated by DampTestSigningOn (unlike IOCTLs 0x226004 and 0x22a008 which require test signing). The handler is reachable whenever the device can be opened. Device access may be restricted by the security descriptor applied via WdmlibIoCreateDeviceSecure (test-signing builds) or default IoCreateDevice security (production builds).
security_findings.primitive:
1. Kernel NULL pointer dereference → Denial of Service (BSOD/bug check). Triggerable from user mode by inducing memory pressure then sending the IOCTL.

2. Kernel object reference leak → Resource exhaustion and object lifetime corruption. The EPROCESS for the target PID can never be freed. In combination with other vulnerabilities, the stale EPROCESS reference could potentially be abused to access freed/reused kernel pool memory (UAF escalation primitive). The leak also causes gradual kernel resource exhaustion proportional to the number of IOCTL calls.
security_findings.assembly:
=== IOCTL 0x22a01c path in DampIoDispatch (UNPATCHED, function @ 0x1c000a3d0) ===

; ---- IOCTL dispatch: subtraction chain over eax = IoControlCode ----
0x1c000a41a: mov     r8d, [rsi+18h]              ; IoControlCode
0x1c000a41e: mov     r9d, 226004h
0x1c000a424: mov     ecx, [rsi+10h]              ; InputBufferLength
0x1c000a427: mov     eax, r8d
0x1c000a42a: mov     edx, [rsi+8]                ; OutputBufferLength
0x1c000a430: mov     rdi, [r14+18h]              ; AssociatedIrp.SystemBuffer (input)
0x1c000a434: sub     eax, r9d                    ; - 0x226004
0x1c000a437: jz      loc_1C000A79A
0x1c000a43d: sub     eax, 10h
0x1c000a440: jz      loc_1C000A748
0x1c000a446: sub     eax, 3FF4h
0x1c000a44b: jz      loc_1C000A79A
0x1c000a451: sub     eax, 14h                    ; cumulative subtrahend = 0x22a01c
0x1c000a454: jz      loc_1C000A592               ; -> IOCTL 0x22a01c handler

; ---- 0x22a01c handler @ loc_1C000A592 ----
0x1c000a592: cmp     ecx, 8                      ; InputBufferLength == 8 ?
0x1c000a595: jnz     loc_1C000A760               ; STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER
0x1c000a59b: xorps   xmm0, xmm0
0x1c000a59e: mov     [rbp+57h+ObjectAttributes.Length], 30h
0x1c000a5a5: movdqu  xmmword ptr [rbp+57h+ObjectAttributes.SecurityDescriptor], xmm0
0x1c000a5aa: mov     [rbp+57h+ObjectAttributes.RootDirectory], r15
0x1c000a5ae: lea     r9, [rbp+57h+ClientId]      ; ClientId
0x1c000a5b2: mov     [rbp+57h+ObjectAttributes.Attributes], 200h
0x1c000a5b9: lea     r8, [rbp+57h+ObjectAttributes]
0x1c000a5bd: mov     [rbp+57h+ObjectAttributes.ObjectName], r15
0x1c000a5c1: lea     rcx, [rbp+57h+ProcessHandle]
0x1c000a5c5: mov     [rbp+57h+ClientId.UniqueThread], r15
0x1c000a5c9: mov     esi, 1FFFFFh                ; PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS
0x1c000a5ce: mov     eax, [rdi]                  ; PID (DWORD from SystemBuffer)
0x1c000a5d0: mov     edx, esi                    ; DesiredAccess
0x1c000a5d2: mov     [rbp+57h+ClientId.UniqueProcess], rax
0x1c000a5d6: call    cs:__imp_ZwOpenProcess
0x1c000a5e2: mov     ebx, eax
0x1c000a5e4: test    eax, eax
0x1c000a5e6: js      loc_1C000A8C2               ; bail if failed

0x1c000a5ec: mov     r8, cs:__imp_PsProcessType
0x1c000a5f3: lea     rax, [rbp+57h+arg_10]       ; &PEPROCESS output
0x1c000a5f7: mov     rcx, [rbp+57h+ProcessHandle]
0x1c000a5fb: mov     edi, 4B6D6144h              ; tag 'DamK'
0x1c000a600: mov     [rsp+0E0h+HandleInformation], r15
0x1c000a605: xor     r9d, r9d                    ; AccessMode = KernelMode
0x1c000a608: mov     [rsp+0E0h+Object], rax
0x1c000a60d: mov     edx, esi                    ; DesiredAccess 0x1FFFFF
0x1c000a60f: mov     r8, [r8]                    ; PsProcessType
0x1c000a612: mov     [rsp+0E0h+Tag], edi
0x1c000a616: call    cs:__imp_ObReferenceObjectByHandleWithTag  ; PEPROCESS -> [rbp+57h+arg_10]
0x1c000a622: mov     ebx, eax
0x1c000a624: test    eax, eax
0x1c000a626: jns     short loc_1C000A63D
0x1c000a628: mov     rcx, [rbp+57h+ProcessHandle]
0x1c000a62c: call    cs:__imp_ZwClose            ; failure path: close handle, then epilogue
0x1c000a638: jmp     loc_1C000A8C2

; *** the PEPROCESS in [rbp+57h+arg_10] is NEVER dereferenced on any path below ***

0x1c000a63d: mov     rcx, [rbp+57h+arg_10]
0x1c000a641: call    cs:__imp_PsGetProcessImageFileName
0x1c000a64d: mov     rdx, rax                    ; SourceString
0x1c000a650: lea     rcx, [rbp+57h+DestinationString]
0x1c000a654: call    cs:__imp_RtlInitAnsiString
0x1c000a660: mov     r8b, 1                      ; AllocateDestinationString
0x1c000a663: lea     rdx, [rbp+57h+DestinationString]
0x1c000a667: lea     rcx, [rbp+57h+UnicodeString]
0x1c000a66b: call    cs:__imp_RtlAnsiStringToUnicodeString
0x1c000a677: mov     ebx, eax
0x1c000a679: test    eax, eax
0x1c000a67b: js      short loc_1C000A628         ; -> ZwClose then epilogue

0x1c000a67d: mov     rcx, [rbp+57h+arg_10]
0x1c000a681: lea     rax, [rbp+57h+NumberOfBytes]
0x1c000a685: xor     r9d, r9d
0x1c000a688: mov     qword ptr [rsp+0E0h+Tag], rax
0x1c000a68d: xor     r8d, r8d
0x1c000a690: xor     edx, edx
0x1c000a692: call    cs:__imp_PsQueryProcessCommandLine   ; query required length
0x1c000a69e: mov     ebx, eax
0x1c000a6a0: cmp     eax, 0C0000004h             ; STATUS_INFO_LENGTH_MISMATCH ?
0x1c000a6a5: jnz     short loc_1C000A6FB

0x1c000a6a7: mov     edx, dword ptr [rbp+57h+NumberOfBytes]   ; len
0x1c000a6aa: mov     r8d, edi                    ; tag 'DamK'
0x1c000a6ad: mov     ecx, 1                      ; PagedPool
0x1c000a6b2: call    cs:__imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag           ; -> rax
0x1c000a6c9: mov     rsi, rax                    ; P (may be NULL)
; *** BUG #2: no test rax,rax / jz - rsi may be NULL ***

0x1c000a6be: mov     r8d, dword ptr [rbp+57h+NumberOfBytes]
0x1c000a6c2: xor     r9d, r9d
0x1c000a6c5: mov     rcx, [rbp+57h+arg_10]       ; PEPROCESS
0x1c000a6cc: lea     rax, [rbp+57h+NumberOfBytes]
0x1c000a6d0: mov     rdx, rsi                    ; P (NULL -> kernel writes through 0x0)
0x1c000a6d3: mov     qword ptr [rsp+0E0h+Tag], rax
0x1c000a6d8: call    cs:__imp_PsQueryProcessCommandLine   ; NULL-deref sink
0x1c000a6e4: mov     ebx, eax
0x1c000a6e6: test    eax, eax
0x1c000a6e8: jns     short loc_1C000A710         ; success -> DampAddProcessToJobObject

; ExFreePoolWithTag(P) - reached on both success and failure, unconditional on P
0x1c000a6ea: mov     edx, edi                    ; tag 'DamK'
0x1c000a6ec: mov     rcx, rsi                    ; P (may be NULL)
0x1c000a6ef: call    cs:__imp_ExFreePoolWithTag
0x1c000a6fb: lea     rcx, [rbp+57h+UnicodeString]
0x1c000a6ff: call    cs:__imp_RtlFreeUnicodeString
0x1c000a70b: jmp     loc_1C000A628               ; -> ZwClose then epilogue

0x1c000a710: mov     rcx, [rbp+57h+arg_10]
0x1c000a714: call    cs:__imp_PsGetProcessSessionId
0x1c000a720: mov     rcx, [rbp+57h+arg_10]       ; Process
0x1c000a728: mov     r9d, eax
0x1c000a730: mov     r8, rsi
0x1c000a733: mov     rax, [rcx+448h]
0x1c000a73f: call    DampAddProcessToJobObject
0x1c000a744: mov     ebx, eax
0x1c000a746: jmp     short loc_1C000A6EA         ; -> ExFreePoolWithTag path

; ---- epilogue ----
0x1c000a8c2: xor     edx, edx                    ; PriorityBoost
0x1c000a8c4: mov     [r14+30h], ebx              ; IRP->IoStatus.Status
0x1c000a8c8: mov     rcx, r14                    ; Irp
0x1c000a8cb: call    cs:__imp_IofCompleteRequest
0x1c000a8d7: mov     eax, ebx
0x1c000a8d9: mov     rbx, [rsp+0E0h+arg_0]
0x1c000a8e1: add     rsp, 0C0h
0x1c000a8e8: pop     r15
0x1c000a8ea: pop     r14
0x1c000a8ec: pop     rdi
0x1c000a8ed: pop     rsi
0x1c000a8ee: pop     rbp
0x1c000a8ef: retn

breakpoints.why: Entry point of the IOCTL dispatcher. Set breakpoint here and check r8d for IoControlCode 0x22a01c to confirm you've hit the vulnerable handler. Also check ecx (rcx) == 8 for InputBufferLength and rdi points to the SystemBuffer containing the attacker-controlled PID.

ZwOpenProcess call. rcx = &Handle, edx = 0x1FFFFF (PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS), r8 = &ObjectAttributes, r9 = &ClientId. The ClientId.UniqueProcess at [r9] contains the attacker-supplied PID. Verify the process opens successfully.

ObReferenceObjectByHandleWithTag call that obtains the leaked EPROCESS reference. After this returns, [rbp+0x77] contains the PEPROCESS pointer. Note that ObDereferenceObject is NEVER called on this pointer in the unpatched code.

ExAllocatePoolWithTag call with NO subsequent NULL check. After return, rax/rsi holds the allocated buffer P. If rax==0 (allocation failure), the code falls through to PsQueryProcessCommandLine at 0x1c000a6d8 with P==NULL, causing a kernel NULL pointer dereference. Use this breakpoint to inject a NULL return (e.g., 'r rax=0' in WinDbg) to trigger the bug without actual memory pressure.

The NULL dereference sink. If P is NULL, PsQueryProcessCommandLine will attempt to write to address 0. Check rdx (2nd parameter) at 0x1c000a6d0 — if rdx==0, this is the bug firing. Expected crash: PAGE_FAULT_AT_INVALID_ADDRESS or BUGCODE 0x50.

ZwClose call at the end of the IOCTL 0x22a01c handler. After this, the code jumps to the epilogue at 0x1c000a8c2 without calling ObfDereferenceObject. Compare with patched binary which calls ObfDereferenceObject(Process) at 0x1c000a6ea.

watch: r8d at DampIoDispatch entry region (loaded from [rsi+18h]) - selects the 0x22a01c handler via the sub eax,14h / jz loc_1C000A592 chain
ecx at 0x1c000a592 - InputBufferLength, must be 8 (DWORD-aligned PID) or the handler rejects with STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER
rdi - AssociatedIrp.SystemBuffer ([r14+18h]); first DWORD is the attacker-supplied PID
[rbp+0x77] after ObReferenceObjectByHandleWithTag (0x1c000a616) - holds the PEPROCESS pointer (v24) that is leaked
rsi/rax after ExAllocatePoolWithTag at 0x1c000a6b2 - buffer pointer P; if 0, the NULL deref follows
rdx (2nd arg) at PsQueryProcessCommandLine 0x1c000a6d8 - if 0, confirms a NULL buffer is being passed
key_offsets: 0x1c000a454: jz loc_1C000A592 - IOCTL 0x22a01c selected (end of the sub eax,14h subtraction chain)
0x1c000a595: jnz loc_1C000A760 - InputBufferLength != 8 rejected (paired with cmp ecx,8 at 0x1c000a592)
0x1c000a5d6: call ZwOpenProcess - opens target process with 0x1FFFFF (PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS)
0x1c000a616: call ObReferenceObjectByHandleWithTag - obtains the leaked EPROCESS reference (no corresponding ObfDereferenceObject)
0x1c000a6b2: call ExAllocatePoolWithTag - allocation with no NULL check on the return (BUG #2)
0x1c000a6d8: call PsQueryProcessCommandLine with P (rsi) as 2nd arg - NULL-deref sink if P==0
0x1c000a62c: call ZwClose - only handle cleanup; ObfDereferenceObject missing (BUG #1)
PATCHED 0x1c000a6ea: call ObfDereferenceObject(Process) - the added fix for the reference leak
debugging_aids.trigger_setup:
1. Open the DAM device: HANDLE h = CreateFileW(L"\\\\.\\DamCtrl", GENERIC_WRITE, 0, NULL, OPEN_EXISTING, 0, NULL); If access denied, the device ACL may restrict access to elevated callers — run as admin or SYSTEM.

2. Prepare input buffer: DWORD pid = GetCurrentProcessId(); (or any valid PID). The 8-byte buffer is: bytes[0..3] = PID (little-endian DWORD), bytes[4..7] = padding (0).

3. Send the IOCTL: DWORD bytesReturned; DeviceIoControl(h, 0x22a01c, &pid, 8, NULL, 0, &bytesReturned, NULL);

4. For NULL deref (DoS): Ensure paged pool exhaustion before step 3. In WinDbg, break at 0x1c000a6b2 (ExAllocatePoolWithTag return) and force rax=0 to simulate allocation failure without needing real memory pressure. Or exhaust paged pool by creating many large paged allocations from an elevated process.

5. For reference leak: Repeat step 3 many times (e.g., 1000+ iterations) against the same PID. Monitor with !process or kernel debugger — the EPROCESS reference count for the target PID will increase by 1 per call and never decrease.
debugging_aids.expected_observation:
NULL POINTER DEREFERENCE: Bug check 0x50 (PAGE_FAULT_IN_NONPAGED_AREA) or 0x7E with faulting address near 0x0. The crash occurs inside PsQueryProcessCommandLine when it attempts to write to the NULL buffer P. The call stack will show DampIoDispatch → PsQueryProcessCommandLine.

REFERENCE LEAK: No immediate crash, but !process <PID> shows an abnormally high reference count (pointer count). Each IOCTL 0x22a01c call increments the EPROCESS PointerCount by 1. After the target process exits, the EPROCESS remains allocated (never freed) due to the leaked reference. Use !pool or dt _EPROCESS to inspect the leaked object. Over time, kernel pool memory is exhausted by accumulated leaked EPROCESS structures.
debugging_aids.notes: The driver is dam.sys (Desktop Activity Moderator), device \Device\DamCtrl with Win32 symlink \??\DamCtrl. DampIoDispatch is registered in DriverEntry for IRP_MJ_CREATE (0), IRP_MJ_READ (2), IRP_MJ_DEVICE_CONTROL (0xe), and IRP_MJ_CLEANUP (0xf). The IOCTL 0x22a01c handler is NOT gated by DampTestSigningOn (unlike IOCTLs 0x226004/0x22a008 which require test signing). The device is created via IoCreateDevice (non-test-signing) or WdmlibIoCreateDeviceSecure (test-signing) — the ACL determines user accessibility. The pool tag is 'DamK' (0x4b6d6144). In the patched binary, r12 replaces r14 as a callee-saved register, and the stack frame is smaller (0x70 vs 0xc0) due to removing the OBJECT_ATTRIBUTES and CLIENT_ID local variables (no longer needed since PsLookupProcessByProcessId takes a PID directly). The unpatched binary's ZwOpenProcess also requests PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS (0x1FFFFF) for both the handle and the object reference — itself an overprivileged pattern that the patch eliminates.

summary: The patch fixes two security bugs in the IOCTL 0x22a01c handler (add-process-to-job) of DampIoDispatch in dam.sys (Desktop Activity Moderator driver). Bug #1 is an EPROCESS reference leak: ObReferenceObjectByHandleWithTag obtains a PEPROCESS reference that is never released via ObfDereferenceObject — each IOCTL call leaks one reference, permanently preventing the process object from being freed. Bug #2 is a missing NULL check on ExAllocatePoolWithTag — if paged pool allocation fails, PsQueryProcessCommandLine dereferences NULL, causing a kernel bug check (DoS). The patch replaces the ZwOpenProcess + ObReferenceObjectByHandleWithTag chain with PsLookupProcessByProcessId, adds ObfDereferenceObject in unified cleanup, adds the NULL check with STATUS_NO_MEMORY return, and restructures error handling with proper NULL guards. A PoC targeting the unpatched version would open \\.\DamCtrl, send IOCTL 0x22a01c with an 8-byte buffer containing a valid PID, and either loop to leak references or induce memory pressure (or patch the return value in a debugger) to trigger the NULL dereference crash.
